headline: Toledo-Lucas County Port Authority moves to finance renovations at Ashland Manor and Covenant House — two more HUD-subsidized complexes entering preservation pipeline
paragraphs: The Toledo-Lucas County Port Authority's board indicated on May 28 that it intends to finance renovations at two HUD-subsidized affordable housing properties: Ashland Manor (2030 Ashland Ave., 188-unit Section 8 complex built in 1972 serving seniors and low-income residents) and Covenant House Apartments (702 N. Erie St., near the Warehouse District). Both properties are expected to change ownership — likely to a mission-aligned housing entity — as part of the deal, with Port Authority bond financing unlocking the renovation capital. The Blade first reported the planned action May 28.

The mechanism mirrors the Port Authority's November 2025 action on Heritage Village — where a board resolution unlocked $43 million for renovating 191 units in the Vistula neighborhood and south Toledo. The Ashland Manor and Covenant House action follows the same model: Port Authority issues bonds backed by project cash flows, at no direct cost to taxpayers, and the renovation capital is deployed without requiring a city or county budget line.
why_it_matters: The Port Authority is quietly becoming the primary engine of Toledo's affordable housing preservation pipeline — Heritage Village in November 2025, Ashland Manor and Covenant House in May 2026. Each action extracts a deteriorating Section 8 property from limbo and puts renovation capital behind it through an off-balance-sheet structure that doesn't compete with city operating-fund priorities. Covenant House's location at 702 N. Erie St. puts it within two blocks of the Huron Yards development cluster, meaning the renovation investment will benefit from — and contribute to — the Warehouse District's rising service demand. Watch for the formal Port Authority board resolution and the identity of the new ownership entity, which will indicate whether this is a for-profit or nonprofit preservation deal.

sources.title: The Blade — Port authority intends to finance renovations at Ashland Manor, Covenant House
WTOL — Port Authority approves $43M plan to renovate nearly 200 affordable housing units in Toledo (Heritage Village precedent)

locations.label: Covenant House Apartments — 702 N. Erie St.
